text: The patient is positioned supine with legs open and arms tucked. Three trocars are inserted: a 12mm trocar in the right upper quadrant and two 5mm trocars in the right flank and right lower quadrant. The laparoscopic exploration reveals an antacolic gastric bypass, dilation of mesenteric lymphatic channels, and increased lymphatic fluid. The bowel is visibly twisted and incarcerated in the mesenteric defect at the JJ anastomosis.

The surgeon reduces the small bowel and uses hook cautery to take down an adhesion. The alimentary limb is run proximally to identify the Peterson space, where the greater omentum and transverse colon are mobilized from each side. The bowel is then run distally to identify the ligament of Treitz. The biliary limb is run until reaching the JJ anastomosis, where the mesenteric opening is closed with a running 3-0 silk suture.
The surgeon runs the alimentary limb until identifying Peterson's defect, which is then closed using a 3-0 silk suture in a running fashion.
